raw_consumer_statement: This whole matter began a week ago Friday when I received a text from Citibank inquring if I recognized a {$28.00} charge. I did not recognize the charge and typed N in the reply. I did not receive any furhter information or notifications from Citibank that they were canceling my card and reissuing a new one to me due to this on letter reply. I was unaware that they had taken this action when I attempted to use the card at the post office the next morning for a {$10.00} purchase which was declined. I immediately worried what was happening, perhaps my card was overdrawn which would have meant someone had been fraudulent or unknown charges were approved. Either way I was distressed and stressed until I could travel back to my home and get on the computer and realize that Citibank had cancelled my card and was reissuing a new one. I immediately called Citibank to express my desire to continue with my current card because I did not want the extra work required to notify all my current auto pay creditors for that card. When I phoned I was serviced by clearly outsourced individuals to another coutnry on behalf of Citibank and explained this to them. At no point did this representative explain to me how simpel and easy this process would be, rather she argued and would not listen to my need to have this accomplsihed. While this was happening I was on line and she was telling me I needed to complete the page that popped up and approve the charges that were needing boxes checked. At no time did Citi help me understand that what I really was checking on these boxes were disputes. They subsequently showed up as fraud disputes and I became of this only after I hit the send button, rather than even a pop up that would say are you sure you want to dispute these acounts. Again with a click XXXX accounts were disputed without my permissoin. I called Citi immediately and said these were not disputed. They refused to remove the dispute notification within XXXX minutes of it being posted. I have been calling Citibank eveyr day over 12 separate occasions to get htis straighted out. I've tried to email the CEO to state my grievances. I've been told by XXXX customer service representatives that they would remove the disputes and authorize the funds to my vendors/merchants. They have done neither to a very high financial devastation to all of them. I am hereby requesting {$4000.00} from Citibank to cover allt he costs and fees of their negligence.
